items.title:
1956-1976: Dartmouth Conference Prediction
The founders of AI predicted that a machine as intelligent
as a human could be built in 'one generation.' The generation
in question has since retired without comment.

1970-1978: Marvin Minsky's Prediction
Minsky predicted machines with the general intelligence of
an average human being within '3 to 8 years.'
The error bars on this estimate have since expanded to include infinity.

1997-2029: Kurzweil's Prediction
Ray Kurzweil predicts AGI by 2029. He has been consistently
updating the definition of AGI to remain technically correct,
which is the best kind of correct when selling books.

2015-2035: Survey of AI Researchers
Multiple surveys of AI researchers converge on '10-20 years'
as the expected timeline. This prediction has itself remained
stable at '10-20 years' for approximately 60 years.

2023-2028: The 'Relatively Soon' Era
Sam Altman and contemporaries predict AGI 'relatively soon,'
a formulation notable for its complete absence of falsifiability.
'Relatively' is doing considerable load-bearing work in this sentence.

1997: Deep Blue Defeats Kasparov
IBM's Deep Blue defeats world chess champion Garry Kasparov.
Immediately reclassified as 'not real AI' by those who had
previously defined chess as the benchmark for intelligence.

2011: Watson Wins Jeopardy!
IBM's Watson defeats champions Ken Jennings and Brad Rutter.
Watson's subsequent career in healthcare has been less successful,
a trajectory familiar to many quiz show winners.

2016: AlphaGo Defeats Lee Sedol
DeepMind's AlphaGo defeats world Go champion Lee Sedol 4-1.
The victory is immediately downgraded to 'just tree search'
by observers who cannot play Go.

2020: GPT-3 Released
OpenAI releases GPT-3, a language model that can generate
convincing text. Critics note it doesn't 'truly understand.'
The same criticism applies to most email correspondence.

2022: ChatGPT Released
ChatGPT reaches 100 million users in two months.
Education administrators begin forming committees
to discuss its implications, a process expected
to conclude around 2047.

2026: Current State
AI systems demonstrate remarkable capabilities in narrow domains.
AGI remains approximately 10-20 years away,
as it has been since 1956.
